PR surface: Source +5, Tests +30. Total +35 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current mainâ€™s `isSilentReplyText` only applies an exact `NO_REPLY` regex, so `<think>...</think>NO_REPLY` cannot match. I did not run tests because this review was read-only.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): strip reasoning blocks before NO_REPLY detection (#66701)
target.body:
## Summary

`isSilentReplyText` in `src/auto-reply/tokens.ts` uses an exact-match regex (`^\s*NO_REPLY\s*$`) to suppress agent replies. Some models can prepend reasoning blocks before the token:

```text
<think>
Cav is talking to someone else. I will stay quiet here.
</think>NO_REPLY
```

The regex fails on this input and the raw reasoning text is posted to chat.

This patch calls `stripReasoningTagsFromText` from `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.ts` before testing the input. That strips supported reasoning tags such as `<think>`, `<thinking>`, and `<antthinking>` before the exact `NO_REPLY` match.

The #19537 behavior is covered by tests: substantive replies that end with `NO_REPLY` are still not suppressed after reasoning-tag stripping.

Closes #66701.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/tokens.ts` â€” import `stripReasoningTagsFromText` and strip reasoning tags before the exact-match check in `isSilentReplyText`
- `src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts` â€” 7 new test cases for reasoning-prefix variants, multiple blocks, token-inside-block, and unclosed-block cases

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `isSilentReplyText("<think>I will stay quiet.</think>NO_REPLY")` returned `false`, which allowed reasoning text to be posted to chat; it now returns `true`.

Real environment tested: unit test suite `src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts`

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/auto-reply/tokens.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix:
```text
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns true when <think> block precedes token (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns true when <think> block with whitespace precedes token (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns true for <antthinking> block prefix (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns false when substantive text follows token after reasoning strip (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns true for multiple sequential reasoning blocks before token (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns false when token is inside a reasoning block with content after (#66701)
âś“ isSilentReplyText > returns false for unclosed reasoning block containing substantive text before token (#66701)
Tests: 31 passed (31)
```

Observed result after fix: all 31 tests pass; the 7 new #66701 cases cover the pure-function fix surface.

What was not tested: live Telegram, Signal, or Slack delivery with a real Gemini or Haiku model producing a reasoning-prefixed `NO_REPLY`. Crabbox proof would cover that full path.
target.author: martingarramon
